task.objective: Enhance the `list_branches` and `list_suites` MCP tools to return additional statistics including pass rate, last execution status, and execution count. Currently these tools only return branch/suite names in a simple list.

context.project_type: E2E Test Dashboard - MCP Server Tools
context.background:
The `list_branches` and `list_suites` tools help AI assistants discover available branches and test suites. However, they only return names, forcing additional calls to get_executions or get_dashboard_metrics to understand health status.
Enhancing these tools with inline statistics enables: - Quick health assessment without follow-up queries - Better prioritization (e.g., "focus on branches with low pass rate") - Reduced API call overhead for common analysis workflows
The optimization analysis suggests adding: - pass_rate: Average pass rate across executions - last_status: Most recent execution status (success/failure) - execution_count: Total runs (already partially supported) - last_run: Most recent execution timestamp
context.audience: - AI coding assistants analyzing test health - Developers checking branch/suite status
context.impact: MEDIUM - Reduces API calls needed for common discovery workflows. Particularly useful for AI triage and branch comparison.

issue.description: Tool description claims last_run and execution_count are returned
issue.actual: Database only returns branch/suite names as string array
issue.fix: Update database functions to return full statistics object

issue.impact: Users cannot assess branch/suite health without additional queries
Always returns all-time data, cannot focus on recent activity

functional.requirement: Return last_run timestamp for each branch/suite
Add optional days parameter to filter recent activity
Sort by last_run descending (most recent first)

non_functional.requirement: Query should use window functions efficiently
No N+1 queries - single database call per tool

solution.approach: 1. Update database functions to return objects with statistics 2. Use aggregation queries with proper filtering 3. Update tool definitions with new fields 4. Update handlers to return enriched objects
solution.types:
interface BranchStatistics { branch: string last_run: string | null execution_count: number pass_rate: number last_status: "success" | "failure" | "running" | null }
interface SuiteStatistics { suite: string last_run: string | null execution_count: number pass_rate: number last_status: "success" | "failure" | "running" | null }

steps.step: 1. Start dev server: npm run dev
2. Call list_branches with no parameters - verify statistics returned
4. Verify last_status is one of success/failure/running
5. Call list_branches with days=7 - verify filtered results

test.script:
# Test 1: Default behavior - branches curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "list_branches", "args": {}}'
# Verify response has pass_rate and last_status fields
# Test 2: With days filter curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "list_branches", "args": {"days": 7}}'
# Test 3: Suites curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "list_suites", "args": {}}'

test.description: Breaking change: Response structure changes from string[] to object[]. Callers expecting string array will need to update. However, tool description already documented object format.

implementation_order.step: Create BranchStatistics and SuiteStatistics type interfaces
Update getBranches function with aggregation query
Update getSuites function with aggregation query

always.rule: Default days to 30 for reasonable scope
Sort by last_run descending (most active first)
Return pass_rate as 0-100 number, not decimal
Use single efficient query with window functions

never.rule: Never make N+1 queries for statistics

documentation_updates.description: The MCP documentation page must be updated to reflect the new parameters and response fields for list_branches and list_suites tools.

update.current_code: const metadataTools = [ { name: "list_branches", description: "Get list of branches with test runs in the last 30 days.", category: "metadata" as const, parameters: [], responseFields: [ "branch: string - Branch name", "last_run: ISO datetime", "execution_count: number", ], }, { name: "list_suites", description: "Get list of test suites with runs in the last 30 days.", category: "metadata" as const, parameters: [], responseFields: [ "suite: string - Suite name", "last_run: ISO datetime", "execution_count: number", ], }, ]
update.updated_code: const metadataTools = [ { name: "list_branches", description: "Get list of branches with test run statistics.", category: "metadata" as const, parameters: [ { name: "days", type: "number", default: "30", description: "Include branches with runs in last N days" }, ], responseFields: [ "branch: string - Branch name", "last_run: ISO datetime - Most recent execution", "execution_count: number - Total runs in period", "pass_rate: number (0-100) - Average success rate", "last_status: success | failure | running - Most recent result", ], }, { name: "list_suites", description: "Get list of test suites with run statistics.", category: "metadata" as const, parameters: [ { name: "days", type: "number", default: "30", description: "Include suites with runs in last N days" }, ], responseFields: [ "suite: string - Suite name", "last_run: ISO datetime - Most recent execution", "execution_count: number - Total runs in period", "pass_rate: number (0-100) - Average success rate", "last_status: success | failure | running - Most recent result", ], }, ]

files_to_modify.file: lib/db.ts - Update getBranches and getSuites functions
lib/mcp/tools.ts - Update tool definitions and handlers

output_format.complexity: Medium - Requires SQL aggregation with subqueries
